text: De-clutter decision-making. Don't say 'maybe' when you mean 'no' — particularly an Indian tendency! Keep channels open and clear for the right opportunities to come your way. Have faith, they will. All the while, keep working towards being undeniable.

If you're generating business, you don't need to be in every Instagram photo and influencer clusterfuck. And if you're not generating business, no amount of photos with fancy people can save you. Focus on the business, making money, and finding your audience and client base.

You're either building a business or you're not — there is no part-timing it. And if you're maniacally chasing a dream, a vision, or a goal — stop apologizing, especially the women, and just go for it full throttle and embrace the madness. The world needs energetic, sparked doers — lord knows there's enough thinkers. Be the person that gets it done.

Perfection is the enemy of progress and ego is the enemy of business. Do whatever it takes to close the deal. No one will care what it took to get things done — all they'll remember is that you got it done.

In America they say 'follow your dreams' — but for many of us, our dreams are unclear. They are limited by real life issues and many of us have no particular dream besides knowing that we want to 'do something.' I say follow your curiosity. Take one tiny manageable step in a new direction and be open to where that leads. Life is about revelations and revealing yourself — don't be in a rush to 'get somewhere.' Enjoy each new move with enthusiasm and believe this ripple will grow and show you the way, and it will.

No one thing can make or break you — let that fear go. Keep taking chances, keep making mistakes, keep getting back on your feet. The world loves watching an underdog win — believe that the world is rooting for your success. Truly believe it. What do you have to lose?

Whatever you want from the universe — make that ask clear and specific to increase your odds of getting it or coming close. For example: 'I wish I'm a big success in the real estate biz' versus 'I want to sell 3 apartments in 2023.' Know that positive forces are working for you but make it easy for them to give you what you want.
When the next step shows up, you're ready! Stop second-guessing yourself and thinking you're not ready — the universe does not issue 'you're ready' certificates. You issue it for yourself and go.

Stop trying to find a way to have it all — every successful person had to make difficult choices. Every single one. To build a new business, you will have to sacrifice — a lot. There's no way around this. Figure out what you need to do, make your peace with the things you have to get rid of, and forge full speed ahead.

Real real trumps orchestrated and scripted real. If you're struggling to jump in the fray — I hope I gave you the confidence to take a shot, and you know I'm living it myself. 90% of my videos are shot in under 5 minutes, no script, no mic, no expensive camera, no drama. Just one fun idea and fast execution — because the kids are going to run away! As the saying goes, don't make an ad, make a TikTok.

I worked on a collab with a creator — sweetest person ever but chairman of overthinkers anonymous. He had a 3-page script for a 30-second TikTok. I watched him bend himself into a pretzel to stick to the script. An hour into the madness, I told him: please put your magic camera down, throw this script out and just talk to me. I held my phone up as he was trying to say it and boom — TikTok done.

No one knows anything. No one. Especially not the experts. So the only way to do anything is by doing the thing and figuring it out one step at a time. As I tell my kids: the long way is the short way.

Building a business requires upfront investment — time, money, physical, emotional — that reflects the size of your belief in the business. You don't plant a seed and sit in the shade of its tree overnight. This is mostly meant as a reminder to me because I'm one inch away from having a full blown panic attack over all the projects I'm creating, but hopefully it helps you too.

I've been saying 'no' a lot more recently to preserve my sanity and I've learned that life goes on. No more 'I'll think about it,' 'I'll get back to you,' 'we'll see' and such — close the chapter and move on. It's best for all involved to minimize the bullshittery.

If your mind feels scattered, terrified, and/or anxious — you are not alone and the only way to stay sane is to take positive action towards a goal. Move more for fitness. Sleep more for calm. Create more for business. Some of the best art has been created during the worst times — put that unrest to work.

I spent many years wondering if there was something else out there for me. The only reason I found my thing is because I kept taking chances and making mistakes until I heard the click in comedy. I've been ridiculed, embarrassed, tormented, and worse through my journey but none of it mattered because I was determined to keep going. Fail fast, fail hard, pivot and keep going. There's no other way. The only way to it is through it.
Remember the man on top of the mountain didn't fall there. If you're feeling sorry for yourself, take a beat, indulge in self-pity for a second and then get back on it.
The only thing that protects you in this crazy world is MONEY. Remember that as you set your priorities.

You can't control many things in life — luck, opportunities, X factor. But you can control EFFORT. Develop a reputation for being the hardest worker in the room. Everybody wants to work with the person who shuts up and does the job well.

I had to remind people in a meeting: without social media you have no pathway to selling this thing that you're creating. None. Good luck 'putting an announcement in the trades' or whatever bullshittery you think you're going to do. I can't believe I'm witnessing a real-life adapt or perish crisis. The insanity of talking to someone in Hollywood who doesn't understand the power of social media. I'm losing my mind listening to total gobbledegook from veterans who are so out of touch it's frightening. And they're talking to ME! My life is creating a super-charged, hyper-activated digital world.

When you're comfortable, you're at risk for obsolescence. If you want a seat on the rocket ship, embrace the discomfort. There's no great mystery to this — every time you feel out of your depth, say to yourself: I WILL STAY IN THE GAME no matter how steep the curve.
Forget everything I've ever said about working and motivation and blah blah. If you can marry rich, just do that.
Cancel hustle and grind. Bring in: do the best you can. Sometimes less is more and being persistent is greater than being perfect.

You know that saying 'it's not personal, it's business'? Remember that when you get rejected from opportunities. And use it to help yourself be persistent and take another shot and another one and another one.

A reputation of 'nice' is great but you're not a leader — or a parent — if you can't make difficult decisions. Business and life is not a popularity contest. Being respected is greater than being liked.

Once you understand how to be a salesperson, you will be unstoppable in everything you do. Get over the hump, the hesitation, and treat selling like it's a sport. Practice with little projects, goods, services, and keep pushing into bigger things. Everybody needs the person who can generate revenue.

NEVER go to a business meeting hungry. When you're scheduled to meet someone for work — often at restaurants, cafes — it's about the work. It's not about checking out the menu, getting into your allergy restrictions, milk-alternative requirements, or your fasting window. Always eat something before showing up and be ready to get into it.
If you're not taking risk, you're not growing the business. If you're not growing the business, you risk stagnating. If you start stagnating, staying in business becomes risky.

What works for accounts that want to grow: 1. Believe that this medium is important — give it the respect it deserves. 2. Authentically share your journey and educate, empower or entertain with each post. 3. Focus on who follows you rather than how many people follow you — attract your tribe for maximum convertibility. And most importantly, ignore the haters — winners are too busy to hate.

A comic reaching a million views on one reel asks: I'm here, now what? Now do it again and again and again and again and again. People mock content creators and influencers as if it's all so easy — in many ways it's the hardest thing because there is no roadmap for it. There is no internship, no mentorship — it's a million leaps of faith combined with a trillion trials, attempts, and fails. And no, you can't just buy followers and likes. When you do that, the whole world knows — you're not fooling anybody except yourself.

Maximal living, not minimal. Don't think: how much do I need to make to live the life I want? That's stress-inducing and leads to a scarcity mindset. Instead ask: how do I maximize the value of every working hour in my day? This number should far surpass whatever you need to live well and help you constantly level up and evolve. Add skills, decline low-paying jobs, reduce the busy-work, and focus on selling more. Increase the top line!

Many of you are stopped by feeling 'cringe' about self-promoting. What you think is self-promoting is actually called marketing. The most recognizable brands do the most marketing — do you think it's a coincidence? It is your job to spread word of your work and invite people into your world relentlessly. Be extra to win extra.

I also live a terrified life because I lost everything — and I mean everything — at 15 and lived the life of being a semi-permanent grifter and beggar for many years thereafter. My book explains more. Honestly I wish I was less worried, but it's not in my destiny. That said — I hope I'm also inspiring people to know that they too can turn it around.

This type of comment comes up periodically: 'I often wonder what happened to your family financially that you seem to be really concerned with money?' My response: 1. I'm an immigrant mother of 3 with a million responsibilities I don't talk about. 2. We chose to make a life in NYC to take moonshots at our ambitions. 3. I also love business and the metric for measurement of success in business is money.
Proceed as if success is inevitable and it will be. Make leaps of faith, dream big dreams, bet on yourself, take that chance. Cliché but true — fortune favors the bold.
In business and in life — be polite, but firm. Say what you mean, stick to your guns, and do it with a smile. People won't take you seriously until you take yourself seriously.

When your business is growing and ascending, a million fools will try to appease you with empty platitudes — do not believe them. The metrics for whether your work is resonating are clear: are your tickets selling, is your account growing, can you get a higher price or better terms? Numbers don't lie. If yes, business is booming. If no, you have a problem. The masters deal with their problems head on. Be a master.

Any business or service that helps you free up your time is worth a high price — as long as you will put that freed-up time to productive use. Take that Uber if you're going to sit in the back seat and work. Order that takeout if you'll use the time not spent cooking on acquiring a new life skill. Hire that housekeeper if having an organized, clutter-free home allows you to get more done.

In business, the number one priority is your relationship with your client, your audience, your customer. Hold that bond sacred and do everything to do right by paying clients. Make decisions that serve them first and foremost. Having clarity on this will help you build faster and stronger. Anything that doesn't fit this agenda has got to go.

After 5 years of grinding hard — next weekend might be my first weekend off. But it might be and I'm already crazed about what I will do or not do. All I really want is to go for a long walk with my husband and kids. It's easy to be a comic, but it's hard — very very hard — to create and sustain a comedy business.
Attention grads — young adulthood is confusing, allow me to simplify: money greater than no money. You're welcome.

Start each day by saying to yourself: 'nothing and no one will stop me.' This will help you overcome and recover from setbacks in your journey — there will be many. Operating from a foregone conclusion shifts your mindset into solution-oriented thinking. Every problem becomes a puzzle piece waiting to be solved — that's all.

When you're afraid to negotiate for yourself, remember that you owe it to your shareholders to get the deal right. Your shareholders are your family, your community, people whose love you benefit from — and they depend on you. Do it for your team.

Don't waste your energy worrying. The thing you worry about likely won't happen. The thing that happens will be a curveball you couldn't have seen coming. Spend your time creating and have faith that things will work out. And it will.

If you're feeling wronged, betrayed, or played — remember every successful person has taken big hits, justified or not, and kept going. Don't compound the loss by wasting time and dwelling on it. Take the L and move on — good things await around the bend.

Sale before scale. Stop worrying whether you have the next unicorn idea and focus on: can you sell one unit of the thing, and what will it cost to do so? A simple business with healthy profit margins will still be standing when the sexy, of-the-moment ideas are gone.

It's hard for everyone. The supermodel still has to work out. The gifted musician still has to practice. The winning athlete still has to deal with defeats. Stop the self-pity party and stay on the grind.

Building a business requires extreme amounts of focus. Decide which battles you'll fight and which you won't get involved in to preserve your power. Be a lion — lazy and chill until it's time to go for the kill.

If you want to succeed in a big way, remove decision fatigue from your life. Eat the same few things, have a quiet boring schedule, create a uniform look and stick to it. Obsessively save energy units for business generation only.

The answer is either yes or no — not maybe. Maybe equals no. Stick to your decision and work out the process later. Doing something without conviction is meaningless anyway — you're better off taking a nap.
You cannot save your way to wealth. Wealth creation requires big, bold moves. Stop sitting by the sidelines and get in the game.
Showing up 5 minutes early to a meeting is a business hack. Get there with a peaceful mind, scope out the lay of the land, resolve any unexpected tangles — position yourself for a win.

Social media, like life, is what you make of it. I'm fully focused on creating a positive, happy, creative space and fortunately attract the very best to my page. Trolls are often disappointed and turn a new leaf once they discover our community. I'm most proud of my robust comment sections where brilliant, passionate people hang out every day and all are welcome.

What is the objective of your business? Make money? Change lives? Create art? Keep you busy? Something else? Figure out the answer to this and make it the North Star for all decisions. Without knowing where you want to go, you'll never get there.
If you're not singularly focused on revenue generation and profitability, whatever you're doing is a hobby, not a business.

As an entrepreneur, founder, or CEO your obligation is to watch out for your business. You have to work with whoever is in office, around whatever the rules are, and whenever your company needs you to. Don't get lost in the hype. Your number one job is to protect and advocate for your business.

Secret hack to land opportunities: build a reputation for being a pinch hitter. Someone who can be brought in last minute, in a moment of crisis, and who will be a good team player and will deliver. So many deals came to me because someone else backed out or fell apart. I have no ego — if it speaks to me, I'll get involved and do everything to deliver a hit.
Learning to work with incompetent, unmotivated, and irresponsible people is a test of leadership. Be calm, polite, firm, and keep your eyes on the bigger picture always.
Everyone is scared, nervous, and unsure. The winners just disregard the noise and go for it anyway. Learning to not fear failure is the single most effective hack in business.

Regardless of who wins the election, you will win at life if you focus on things within your control. Everything else is just noise and distraction. No one will die without your opinion but your business might get killed by your sermonizing. Is that what you want — to be right, but unsuccessful?
It's not that you won't get what you don't ask for — it's that you won't get what you don't fight for. No one has it easy.

Over-promise and then over-deliver. It can be done. Go above, beyond, and then some for your partners, clients, producers, and collaborators with no regard for what you got paid for. Trust that it will all come back to you. Don't just aim to deliver — aim to delight.

Shut that laptop and get out in the real world. Take random shots in life. Increase the surface area for serendipity to strike. Raise your odds of getting lucky by doing lots of things. He who dares, wins.

The face of poverty in America is motherhood. The number one demographic pushed out of the workforce is mothers. The lowest paid demographic relative to peers is mothers. The least likely to be hired is women with children. Roll up your sleeves and get in the game, moms. Let this be the year mothers enter the chat and remind every person on earth where they came from.

Hindi phrase: 'pratyaksh ko pramaan kya' — in English, 'presence does not require proof.' In the law and in Latin: 'res ipsa loquitur' — the thing speaks for itself. Make this the year of the mic drop. Results that end all conversations.
Nothing matters more than your determination to win. It's not the size of the dog in the fight, it's the size of the fight in the dog. Don't let anything or anybody stand in the way of what's yours.
All you need to get started as an entrepreneur is ONE paying customer for your product or service. It's not that serious. It's not that hard. And it's not too late.
All the hype in the world won't save bad work. All the hate in the world won't keep good work down. Do the work.

The number one way to build a successful business is to focus on small, tiny sales. It's not the perfect product, not the best marketing, and certainly not the ideal business plan. Sales. Get the sales momentum going and use each sale to get the next one.

If you wanna play the game, you've gotta get in the arena and get close to the action. You can't dial it in, you can't remote your way to the top, and you certainly can't decide 'after something happens.' Your faith and belief in yourself has to show up first and then persist against every naysayer. Are you in or are you out?

Business is a game of persistence, not proof. We know you can do it — but can you do it over and over and over consistently, through all of life's ups and downs? It's a marathon, not a sprint — plan accordingly.
When opportunity presents itself, say YES! Work out the details later. Have a bias towards action even if it means failing and getting up and failing again. Movement is greater than staying put.
Business hack: smile, have a cheery disposition. Enthusiasm is contagious. If you're not excited about your own idea, why will anyone else be?

One business and life skill to learn above all else: SALES. The perfect product or service is irrelevant if you're not willing to go to war to sell it. And no, this cannot be outsourced — YOU have to sell your idea. All day, every day, everywhere you go.
You don't need to know anyone. People need to know you for the work that you do. Networking is out. Creating is in.
Things go wrong in business all the time. How you handle those turns will define your success. Grace is greater than anger always. The moment will come and go — feelings will last forever.

Define the goal to increase your odds of meeting it. I gave my daughter Zoya the job of generating 10,000 book pre-orders by launch date. Not 'do the best you can,' not 'do what feels right' — the goal is 10,000. A business needs numbers. Anything else is a hobby.

In business and in life, the slaps will keep coming. Learn to rest and not quit. The recovery time needs self-care, indulgence, and slowing down — but that's an investment in your next step. Go crazy taking care of yourself so you can resurface and take care of the problem.
When in doubt, dress up rather than dress down. Extra with enthusiasm will always win over apathetic with cynicism.
People will reject you. Forgive them their immaturity. And repeat to yourself: NOTHING AND NOBODY WILL STOP ME. And keep going.

Do NOT talk about your great idea. Only rookies do that. If you want to impress someone, show them how quickly you took an idea through creation, concept, completion, and delivery. How fast and well can you ship? Everything else is lore.

It is not anyone's responsibility or obligation to help you. The sooner you understand that, the sooner you'll be on a path to victory. If someone chooses to lend you a hand, let that be a happy surprise. But don't waste a second waiting for it.

Be nice to people. It's a good business strategy. No one wants to work with an asshole. Business is personal relationships — it's who you meet with, who you talk to, whose word you trust. Kindness goes a long way.

Don't get pulled in by short-term greed. Create situations where all parties in a transaction get value. You make money, your collaborators make money, and the client finds it worth the money. Play long-term games with long-term people and build something real.
